Issue #24600: `CCLOpConfig` Struct Assumes i2i or s2s
------------------------------------------------------------
In the constructor of `CCLOpConfig`, the `shard_grid_size` field is initialized as follows: 

`shard_grid_size(output_tensors.at(0).is_sharded() ? input_tensors.at(0).shard_spec()->num_cores() : 0),`

This logic is incorrect if the op is doing an i2s or s2i (which is likely to become more prevalent via the `ShardedAddrGen`):
- **s2i**: `shard_grid_size` is initialized to 0 even though the input tensor is sharded.
- **i2s**: non-deterministic seg-fault. Since the output tensor is sharded, it tries to access the input `shard_spec`, which doesn't exist since the input tensor is interleaved, leading to ND seg-fault which depends on what garbage value is read while trying to access the input `shard_spec`.

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24590: Mesh graph descriptor refactoring
------------------------------------------------------------
Discussed with @tt-asaigal that there are several confusing pieces about the current mesh graph descriptros:
1.  Need to rename "Board" to something like "physical resource descriptor" (or something shorter / more catchy). The reason is that it isn't really a "board", rather it corresponds to a physical resource type, and there could be multiple of them. E.g. it is strange to see 3 "boards" ("2x2", "2x1", and "1x1") while being on a Loudbox - I'd expect to either have one 2x4 T3K board or 4 2x1 N300 "boards".
2. "Board" / <something else> should have "ID" not "name", and Mesh should refer to them by IDs. It isn't clear that "name" has any significance, this reads like a human-readable string for debugging.
3. Currently, Mesh refers back to Board, but Host assignment is declared "backwards" from within Mesh. Can we have a hierarchical layer that specifies "Host" that links to Mesh just like Mesh links to Board?
4. Currently, file names have meaning, and `ControlPlane` makes decisions based on this (see example below). This should be based on "type" of the mesh graph descriptor, and moved into the yaml file itself.

```cpp
 else if (
        mesh_graph_desc_filename == "t3k_mesh_graph_descriptor.yaml" ||
        mesh_graph_desc_filename == "n150_mesh_graph_descriptor.yaml" ||
        mesh_graph_desc_filename == "n300_mesh_graph_descriptor.yaml" ||
        mesh_graph_desc_filename == "n300_2x2_mesh_graph_descriptor.yaml" ||
        mesh_graph_desc_filename == "multihost_t3k_mesh_graph_descriptor.yaml") {
        // Pick out the chip with the lowest ethernet coordinate (i.e. NW chip)
        const auto& chip_eth_coords =
            tt::tt_metal::MetalContext::instance().get_cluster().get_all_chip_ethernet_coordinates();
        TT_FATAL(!chip_eth_coords.empty(), "No chip ethernet coordinates found in ethernet coordinates map");
```

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24589: Convenience descriptor type for multi-host mesh topology
------------------------------------------------------------
Need a convenience type for describing global shape, local shape + local offset of a mesh. This should ideally be query-able off mesh device, but no reason this can't be initialized on its own.

This came up in several places recently:
1. `DistributeHostBuffer::create`. It is currently accepting 3 parameters explicitly; there is also overload that just accepts "shape", in case the mesh is not multi-host.
2. `from_host_shards` API that uses (1). It is currently limited to single-host.
3. `allocate_tensor_on_host`. Prototype in https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/23865 uses `MeshDevice` to determine the shape at this moment.
4. A workaround in the old serialization code uses `MeshDevice`. Hopefully we will remove it sooner than later though.

@cfjchu mentioned in one of the prototypes this is already available. This needs to be exposed in TT-NN public APIs.

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24587: [Blackhole] Incorrect results when using untilize with dtype=bfloat16
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

When running VGG-family models with dtype=bfloat16 in tt-torch, a PCC error occurs on Blackhole devices. The same models work correctly on Warmhole.

Upon investigation, the first 32 elements of the output appear correct, but all subsequent values are filled with zeros. If I change the untilize operation in the tt-mlir runtime to run on the host instead of the device, the issue does not occur and the outputs are correct.

However, when I create a unit test using the same data type and shape, the issue does not occur. It seems to happen only with these specific models, so a full model analysis may be necessary to pinpoint the root cause.

One particularly odd aspect is that when I inspect the intermediate results of ops using the runtime's hooking feature, the output from the final add layer seems to be valid. In other words, it seems that the issue occurs only at the very last output.

For now, I will share only the TTNN graph for the failing case. For reference, I will also provide the TTNN graph for a unit test that works correctly.

Issue #24582: Unify single-/multi-device sharding concepts
------------------------------------------------------------
- `DistributionMode` and `ShardDistributionStrategy` both describes how to distribute shards (eg. in row-major or within a submesh). Single-device allows round-robin distribution for row-major, which means it allows multiple-shards per bank

Issue #24578: Remove is_size_pow2 templated variants of get_interleaved_addr_gen
------------------------------------------------------------
These variants were added to support outdated use cases of trying to manually pick optimal addr gens in kernels, which passed `is_size_pow2`.

Since `is_size_pow2` is a compile time value, there is almost no reason page size can't be a compile time value as well, in which case the most optimal fn
```
template <bool DRAM, uint32_t page_size>
FORCE_INLINE auto get_interleaved_addr_gen(uint32_t base_addr)
```
should be used.

The handling of `page_size` and `log2_page_size` is very unintuitive in these `is_size_pow2` templated fns, and these functions provide potentially less optimal selections compared to the page_size templated fn above.

We should update any use case of these and then remove these apis.

Only potential niche case I can see is if the kernel is cached on whether pages are power of 2, and allows for runtime page size. Not sure if we have any such use case in the codebase.

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24571: Need uint8 support for ttnn.identity op
------------------------------------------------------------
ttnn.identity op (similar to a copy op) doesn't process uint8 well. The output tensor contains zeros.

Issue #24563: The `ttnn.rand` operation generates values out of the expected range
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

The `ttnn.rand` operation for bfloat16 and all integer types currently generates values that are out of the expected range and do not follow a uniform distribution.

The existing unit tests do not verify the value distribution, which is why this issue was not detected earlier.

Also, the `ttnn.rand` operation hangs for TILE layout with integer data types during Tensor::write_to_string()/MeshCommandQueueBase::enqueue_read() execution.

We need to:

- Add proper support for these data types in `ttnn.rand`
- Ensure that the generated values follow a uniform distribution as expected
- Extend unit tests to validate output distribution for correctness

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Code to reproduce the issue
```
import ttnn
shape=[4,4]


device = ttnn.open_device(device_id=0)

dtype = ttnn.bfloat16
low = 0
high = 1
layout=ttnn.ROW_MAJOR_LAYOUT

tensor = ttnn.rand(shape, dtype=dtype, device=device, low=low, high=high, layout=layout)
print(tensor) # Error: value distribution is not uniform
```

### Expected behavior

Random values must be uniformly distributed

### Please complete the following environment information

OS: Ubuntu 22.04
Python: 3.10.12 
Version: main branch(commit 602333a71b90c4)

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24535: Add "sync-group" concept to fabric test infrastructure for more precise benchmarking
------------------------------------------------------------
Given that different devices can have programs launched at relatively different times, naive start/end measurements of workers in BW/latency tests would not be accurate.

This is because workers could send a non-trivial amount of their data while others have not started, which would incorrectly remove congestion in the router from a portion of the test time - artificially leading to better reported performance.

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24529: tt-metal container image doesn't start SSH on restart
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

If a `tt-metal` container comes up, but then restarts for any reason, SSH is not brought up with it.

Because of this, we've seen a lot of confusion when users try to reconnect to their session.

I will be fast following opening this issue with a PR

### Steps to reproduce the issue

N/A as this is handled through a wrapper (`ird`)

Here is an example however:
```sh
ssh -p 44692 my-tt-metal
ssh: connect to host my-tt-metal port 44692: Connection refused
```

After exec-ing into the container and running `service ssh start`, the user was able to once again connect.

### Expected behavior

Container restarts and SSH is still available

### Please complete the following environment information

_No response_

Issue #24526: Experiment: When IRAM enabled on WH, use erisc kernel binary region in L1 for buffering
------------------------------------------------------------
After the kernel is running out of IRAM, it should be safe to use the L1 space where the kernel binary is stored for channel buffering. This is 16 KB of available space

When IRAM is enabled, reditect one of the channels to this region. Given this is only 16KB, it's  only going to be large enough for a full channel in 2D. 

Therefore, a new way to specify channel addresses will be needed. 

Channel buffer slot addresses are computed at kernel start and cached - they don't need to be calculated dynamically as we move data through the queue. Additionally, because there is a max packet size, this means there is not requirement for adjacent buffer slots to be contiguous in L1!

Therefore, we may decide instead to just calculate all buffer slot addresses on host and pass them in individually to the kernel through compile time args. This will let us use memory in packet-size sized chunks.

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24511: ttnn.where implicitly takes dtype of predicate tensor
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

`ttnn.where` op is implemented as combination of `multiply` and `add` ops. Result dtype is implicitly derived from first operand which is predicate tensor. 
From compiler we'd typically invoke `ttnn.where` with (int32, bf16, bf16) types and expect result to be floating point as well.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
diff --git a/tests/ttnn/unit_tests/operations/eltwise/test_ternary.py b/tests/ttnn/unit_tests/operations/eltwise/test_ternary.py
index 1a2da13363..fe2583c7b1 100644
--- a/tests/ttnn/unit_tests/operations/eltwise/test_ternary.py
+++ b/tests/ttnn/unit_tests/operations/eltwise/test_ternary.py
 ```

Issue #24497: Standardize Cluster Mock Infrastructure for testing without hardware
------------------------------------------------------------
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

The current testing infrastructure for the ControlPlane class relies on virtual methods that are marked as "hacks" in the codebase. Specifically, the `ControlPlane` class exposes virtual methods like `get_adjacent_chips()` and `get_user_exposed_chip_ids()` that are overridden in test classes like `TestingMockControlPlane`. This approach has several problems:

1. **Poor separation of concerns**: The production `ControlPlane` class contains virtual methods solely for testing purposes, which violates clean architecture principles
2. **Tight coupling**: Tests are directly coupled to the internal implementation details of `ControlPlane`
3. **Maintenance burden**: Changes to the production code require updates to test mocks
4. **Limited reusability**: The current mock approach is specific to `ControlPlane` and doesn't provide a standardized way to test other cluster-dependent components

The FIXME comments in the code explicitly state: "this is a hack to get the adjacent chips for testing. We should not expose this to the user. Should look into making a mock cluster object that can be used to test the control plane."

Relevant PR: https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/24305

### Describe the solution you'd like

Create a standardized mock cluster infrastructure that allows testing without hardware dependencies. This should include:

1. **MockCluster Interface**: A standardized interface that can be injected into components that depend on cluster functionality
2. **Configurable Mock Implementations**: Mock implementations that can be configured with test-specific topology and connectivity data
3. **Dependency Injection**: Refactor `ControlPlane` and other cluster-dependent components to accept a cluster interface rather than directly accessing `MetalContext::instance().get_cluster()`
4. **Test Utilities**: Helper classes and utilities to easily create mock clusters with common topologies (mesh, torus, etc.)

**Example of desired usage:**
```cpp
// Instead of current approach with virtual methods
class TestingMockControlPlane : public ControlPlane {
    std::vector<chip_id_t> get_adjacent_chips(chip_id_t chip_id, std::uint32_t num_ports_per_side) const override {
        // Mock implementation
    }
};

// New approach with dependency injection
class MockCluster : public ICluster {
    void configure_topology(const std::unordered_map<chip_id_t, std::vector<chip_id_t>>& adjacency_map);
    std::vector<chip_id_t> get_adjacent_chips(chip_id_t chip_id) const override;
    // ... other cluster methods
};

// ControlPlane accepts cluster interface
class ControlPlane {
public:
    ControlPlane(const std::string& mesh_graph_desc_yaml_file, std::shared_ptr<ICluster> cluster);
    // Consider creating a MockMetalContext with a MockCluster
    // No virtual methods needed for testing
};
```

### Describe alternatives you've considered

1. **Keep current virtual method approach**: This maintains the status quo but doesn't solve the architectural issues and continues to expose testing concerns in production code.

2. **Use existing UMD MockChip infrastructure**: While UMD has `MockChip` and mock cluster capabilities, these are designed for device-level testing and don't provide the topology and connectivity abstractions needed for fabric control plane testing.

3. **Create test-specific ControlPlane subclasses**: This would still require virtual methods and doesn't provide a reusable solution for other components.

4. **Use dependency injection with real cluster**: This would require hardware for all tests, which defeats the purpose of having fast, isolated unit tests.


### Additional context

**Current Implementation Issues:**

The existing `TestingMockControlPlane` class demonstrates the current problematic approach:

```cpp
// From tests/tt_metal/tt_fabric/fabric_router/test_control_plane_logical_to_physical.cpp
class TestingMockControlPlane : public ControlPlane {
private:
    std::unordered_map<chip_id_t, std::vector<chip_id_t>> adjacency_data_;
    std::set<chip_id_t> user_exposed_chip_ids_;

public:
    std::vector<chip_id_t> get_adjacent_chips(chip_id_t chip_id, std::uint32_t num_ports_per_side) const override {
        auto it = adjacency_data_.find(chip_id);
        if (it != adjacency_data_.end()) {
            return it->second;
        }
        return {};
    }

    std::set<chip_id_t> get_user_exposed_chip_ids() const override {
        if (!user_exposed_chip_ids_.empty()) {
            return user_exposed_chip_ids_;
        }
        return {0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7};
    }
};
```

**Existing UMD Mock Infrastructure:**

The UMD layer already has some mock capabilities:
- `MockChip` class for device-level mocking
- `ClusterOptions` with `ChipType::MOCK` for creating mock clusters
- `tt_ClusterDescriptor::create_mock_cluster()` for creating mock cluster descriptors

However, these are focused on device I/O and don't provide the topology and connectivity abstractions needed for fabric control plane testing.

No linked pull requests found.

2. following the install instructions option B from [metal install page.: Option B: Manual Build with CMake](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/blob/main/INSTALLING.md#source) run: 
`mkdir build`
`cd build`
`cmake .. -G Ninja -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=clang++-17 -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=RelWithDebugInfo`
`ninja`

Issue #24489: profiler: kernel cache and device profiler do not work together
------------------------------------------------------------
With kernel caching new metal processes regenerate the zone source location file but because kernels are cached and not compiled anymore source locations can be lost for subsequent run.

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24488: Di/dt issues on IRD machines.
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

I was debugging Qwen3 hang issues, in the process i ran di/dt tests on some of the IRD machines and a cloud machine. Surprizeling i found many machine on which IRD tests hang. 

```
Here is the table i created to summaries:

+----------------------------+--------------------------------------+-------------------+
|          Machine           |              di/dt test              |       qwen3       |
+----------------------------+--------------------------------------+-------------------+
| Cloud machine f09cs04      |  di/dt hangs after 24359 iterations  |  Pass  [1]        |
| IRD machine Sjc-snva-t3018 |  di/dt hangs after 138023 iterations |  Didn't ran qwen3 |
| IRD machine Sjc-lab-t3002  |  di/dt hangs after 11617 iterations  |  Pass  [2]        |
+----------------------------+--------------------------------------+-------------------+
di/dt test command: WH_ARCH_YAML=wormhole_b0_80_arch_eth_dispatch.yaml pytest tests/didt/test_ff1_matmul.py::test_ff1_matmul -k "without_gelu and 8chips" --didt-workload-iterations 250000

[1] = tt-inference-server in docker as server + tt-inference-server's run.py as client. This is baremetal machine
[2] = online vllm server + tt-inference-server's run.py as client. This is IRD machine.
client side command for both [1] and [2]: python run.py --model Qwen3-32B --device t3k --workflow evals
```


On top of this, https://github.com/tenstorrent/cloud/issues/4749 is created by @roseli-TT , when i found di/dt test failed to start on f10cs09.

Can someone check why so many IRD machines are failing with di/dt tests?

CC: @bkeith-TT , @tt-rkim , @uaydonat 


### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Go to '...'
2. Click on '....'
3. Scroll down to '....'
4. See error


### Expected behavior

_No response_

Issue #24487: Average Pool Large Kernel FP32 DST Accumulation
------------------------------------------------------------
Currently for large kernels average pool uses a temporary CB to store intermediate reduction results.  This requires storage of the intermediate results in float16 which reduces precision.  It is possible to instead accumulate results in DST in float32 which will improve precision and most likely performance for small stick sizes, though this will lower max_tiles_per_reduction to 4 instead of 8, so performance may be reduced for wider tensors.

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24485: Look into create_op_performance_model usage for matmul
------------------------------------------------------------
Repro steps:

1. Save a python test with matmul in it to a file. E.g.
```
import pytest
import torch

import ttnn
from tests.ttnn.utils_for_testing import assert_with_pcc

def test_mm4(device, use_program_cache):
    torch.manual_seed(0)
    n = 320
    k = 320
    m = 320
    x = torch.rand([n, k])
    w = torch.rand([k, m])
    result = torch.matmul(x, w)

    tt_x = ttnn.from_torch(x, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, device=device)
    tt_w = ttnn.from_torch(w, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, device=device)
    # Should use compute_kernel_config = WormholeComputeKernelConfig(math_fidelity=HiFi4,math_approx_mode=0,fp32_dest_acc_en=1,packer_l1_acc=0,dst_full_sync_en=0) (device_operation.hpp:221)
    tt_result = ttnn.matmul(tt_x, tt_w) # first call should be a cache miss since  nothing in cache
    ttnn_result = ttnn.to_torch(tt_result)
    assert_with_pcc(result, ttnn_result)

    compute_kernel_config = ttnn.WormholeComputeKernelConfig(
            math_fidelity=ttnn.MathFidelity.LoFi,
            math_approx_mode=True,
            fp32_dest_acc_en=False,
            packer_l1_acc=True,
        )
    tt_result2 = ttnn.matmul(tt_x, tt_w, compute_kernel_config=compute_kernel_config) # cache miss since different compute kernel config from the first call
    ttnn_result2 = ttnn.to_torch(tt_result2)
    assert_with_pcc(result, ttnn_result2, pcc=0.99)
    tt_result3 = ttnn.matmul(tt_x, tt_w, compute_kernel_config=compute_kernel_config) # cache hit since the same as the second call
    ttnn_result3 = ttnn.to_torch(tt_result3)
    assert_with_pcc(result, ttnn_result3, pcc=0.99)
```

2. Profile the test
```
tt_metal/tools/profiler/profile_this.py -c 'pytest "test_mm4.py"'
```

Expected:
- create_op_performance_model in ttnn/cpp/ttnn/operations/matmul/device/matmul_op.cpp functions are called
Actual:
- create_op_performance_model in ttnn/cpp/ttnn/operations/matmul/device/matmul_op.cpp are not called (verified by adding logging)

See https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/issues/18238#issuecomment-2985359571 for details about path through other files that should be taken to reach this code, especially:
- ttnn/api/ttnn/operation.hpp
- ttnn/api/tools/profiler/op_profiler.hpp

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24469: CLIP score is bad with DP on N300
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

## Problem description

When running the SDXL accuracy test on the N300 machine (with DataParallel), the computed **CLIP score** is significantly lower than expected, even though the generated images are visually correct.

After investigation, the problem seems to be:
 
- The order of generated images is shuffled.
- The list of prompts remains unchanged and in correct order.
- As a result, images are evaluated against **mismatched prompts**, which leads to incorrect CLIP scores.

---

## Expected behavior

Each generated image should be evaluated against the **prompt it was generated from**, regardless of the underlying parallelism or hardware setup.

---

## Tasks

- [x] Ensure the mapping between image and prompt is preserved after generation

---

## Notes

- The bug is **not caused by poor image generation**, but rather by incorrect evaluation.
- This only seems to occur when using DP>1 (on N150 we don't have this problem)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

use this command for running accuracy test on **N300**:
```
pytest models/experimental/stable_diffusion_xl_base/tests/test_sdxl_accuracy.py --start-from=0 --num-prompts=10 -k "host_vae"
```

in **output/** folder you can see generaded images which are good, but in **test_reports/sdxl_test_results.json** you will see CLIP score being much worse than expected (it should be in 29-30 range)

### Expected behavior

Each generated image should be compared to the exact prompt it was generated from.


### Please complete the following environment information

_No response_

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24460: Single core Width and Block sharding conv hang
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

`ttnn.conv2d` hangs when single core in Block or Width sharding. 
This occurs due to multicast in the reader kernel, 1 sender, 0 receivers.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Checkout `astancov/bs-ws-singlecore-hang-repro`
2. Run `pytest tests/ttnn/nightly/unit_tests/operations/conv/test_conv2d.py::test_conv_single_core_hang`


### Expected behavior

The test should pass with no hangs.

### Please complete the following environment information

-  N150 board

Issue #24453: Have NOC sanitizer check for open linked transaction when sending a unicast transaction.
------------------------------------------------------------
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

# Problem description
If NOC_CMD_VC_LINKED is set on any command buffer, the next transaction must be an mcast on the same VC. If not, the NOC will completely fail to initiate any new transactions.

See #23095 for an attempt to check for that.  This does not work because in some cases, the linked transaction is opened and closed on different cmd buffers, and NOC_CMD_VC_LINKED will stay set on the first cmd buffer.  We may need to use separate per-NOC storage to explicitly record the state of open linked transactions.

### Describe the solution you'd like

_No response_

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24432: Cannot load Gemma3 weights with transformers>=4.52.0
------------------------------------------------------------
Nested models are rearranged in transformers with commit https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/commit/17742bd9c8852ab35986dcaa3e68415342ae7eef, which is available from v4.52.0.

We need to follow-up with changes in our checkpoints loading.

When I set the last dim to 4, I get this error:
```
E       RuntimeError: TT_FATAL @ /localdev/mradosavljevic/tt-metal/ttnn/core/tensor/layout/tensor_layout.cpp:110: (physical_shard_shape.height() % tile_shape[0] == 0 && physical_shard_shape.width() % tile_shape[1] == 0)
E       info:
E       Physical shard shape (512, 4) must be tile {32, 32} sized!
```

This happens on a customer model.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Here is the code used to repro:
```
import pytest
import torch
import ttnn
@pytest.mark.parametrize("mesh_device", [2], indirect=True)
@pytest.mark.parametrize("shape", [(2,1,16384, 4)])
def test_dist(mesh_device, shape):
    t = torch.rand(shape)
    tt = ttnn.from_torch(t, device=mesh_device, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, mesh_mapper=ttnn.ShardTensorToMesh(mesh_device, dim=0))
    mem_config = ttnn.create_sharded_memory_config(
            shape=(2, 1, 16384, 32),
            core_grid=ttnn.CoreGrid(y=8, x=8),
            strategy=ttnn.ShardStrategy.HEIGHT,
            orientation=ttnn.ShardOrientation.ROW_MAJOR,
        )
    tt = ttnn.to_memory_config(tt, mem_config)
    tt_together = ttnn.all_gather(tt, dim=0, memory_config=mem_config)
```
This is on N300.
I also noticed there is a new ttnn.experimental.all_gather_async op but couldn't figure out how to use it.

Issue #24425: Last dimension in the padded shape of width sharded tensors is incorrect.
------------------------------------------------------------
Padded last dimension should be `num_cores * shard_shape[-1]`. But current tensor infra calculates the padded shape by using Alignment + Logical shape. 
This leads to the `tensor.padded_shape()` being incorrect.

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24418: Enable submesh-to-submesh CCLs
------------------------------------------------------------
There are some use cases where you want to use submeshes for a graph of computation and then share the results between submeshes. This is currently not possible without doing trip through host memory.

I'd like to request a feature such that it is possible to do submesh-to-submesh communication over fabric.

Example
```python
  in0 = torch.randn(logical_shape)
  in1 = torch.randn(logical_shape)
  sm0_in0 = ttnn.from_torch(
      in0,
      device=submeshes[0],
      mesh_mapper=ttnn.ShardTensor2dMesh(submeshes[0], dims=(2, 3), mesh_shape=submesh_shape),
  )
  sm1_in1 = ttnn.from_torch(
      in1,
      device=submeshes[1],
      mesh_mapper=ttnn.ShardTensor2dMesh(submeshes[1], dims=(2, 3), mesh_shape=submesh_shape),
  

  def mesh_to_mesh_communication(sm0_in0, sm1_in1):
      host_sm0_in0 = sm0_in0.cpu()
      host_sm1_in1 = sm1_in1.cpu()
      sm1_in0 = host_sm0_in0.to(submeshes[1])
      sm0_in1 = host_sm1_in1.to(submeshes[0])
      return sm0_in1, sm1_in0

sm0_in1, sm1_in0 = mesh_to_mesh_communication(sm0_in0, sm1_in1)
```

Full test: https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/commit/0753aa3b71ed3a899c44769230be66fdff918c30

In limited testing I found that I get ~2 GB/s when going through host. On Fabric, I'd expect to be able to reach up to 400GB/s (bidir cross section of 8x4 galaxy).

Setting to P1 since I found that submesh-to-submesh is not yet performance critical for my use case but I'd like to prefetch this work and ensure it's in mind.

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24413: Proper fixes for workarounds for FD over fabric port
------------------------------------------------------------
To unblock the porting activity, we had to put some workarounds in place. We need to get rid of them in the long-term and replace with proper fixes. This issue captures all such workarounds:

1. Assuming dispatch routing plane is the last available.
2. Updating the ctx switch interval for the last routing plane in topology.cpp when building fabric kernels
3. Not reserving fabric routers b/w mmio devices on the dispatch routing plane. (On T3K e.g., there are some tests that rely on the availability of 2 links b/w all the inner mmio devices)

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24407: Performance improvement tracker for ttnn.all_to_all_dispatch
------------------------------------------------------------
### Proposed Performance-Improvement Features for `ttnn.all_to_all_dispatch`

Running with Batch = 8 per device and S = 1. Tested on a 6U Galaxy.

| #  | Improvement (short name)              | What it entails                                                                                          | Dev effort | ETA       | PR Link                                                                                     | Theoretical Time (μs) | Actual Time (μs) | Notes        |
|----|---------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------|------------------|--------------|
| 0  | **Initial (no optimizations)**        | Baseline dispatch time before any performance features were added.                                       | —          | —          |                                                                                             | 572.11                 | 553.76           | Baseline     |
| 1  | **Skip duplicate token writes**       | Maintain `[T,D]` one-hot buffer (0 → 1 once token sent to device) to avoid re-sending.                   | *Moderate* | July 14   | [#24816](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/24816)                                | 92.11                  | 97.68            | ✅ Complete  |
| 2  | **Max-size metadata packets**         | Send entire metadata tensor in a max-sized packet. One packet for decode.                                | *Minor*    | July 14   | [#24816](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/24816)                                | 74.96                  | 76.47            | ✅ Complete  |
| 3  | **Fabric 1D support**                 | Add support for 1D fabric routing. Cuts bandwidth usage in half.                                         | *Moderate* | July 18   | [#24873](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/24873)                                | 46.43                  | 53.61            | ✅ Complete  |
| 4  | **Link-split token packets**          | Use multiple links & balanced packet splitting (`min(max(packets_per_link))`).                           | *Moderate* | July 21   | [#25124](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/25124)                                | —                      | —                | ✅ Complete                       |
| 5  | **Link-split metadata packets**       | Split metadata sends when the token count is large (>≈ 250 for deepseek).                                | *Minor*    | July 21   | [#25124](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/25124)                                | 17.04         | 26.93            | ✅ Complete |
| 6  | **Ring topology**                     | Use ring routing to cut latency/bandwidth for token & metadata traffic.                                  | *Moderate* | August 4  |                                                                                             | 11.36                  | —                |              |
| 7  | **Skip extra loops in expert-parallel “all-to-some”** | Add optional parameter describing the expert-token matrix (multihot by default, optional one-hot).       | *Minor*    | TBD       |                                                                                             | —                      | —                |              |
| 8  | **Use `[1, 1, E, D[E]]` layout**      | Replace one-hot with a compact tensor where `D[E]` = devices per expert.                                 | *Minor*    | TBD       |                                                                                             | —                      | —                |              |
| 9  | **Shard metadata tensor onto L1**     | Keep metadata in L1 to remove NOC reads.                                                                 | *Minor*    | TBD       |                                                                                             | —                      | —                |              |
| 10 | **Mcast metadata dispatch**           | Switch all-to-all metadata from many unicasts to multicast.                                              | *Minor*    | TBD       |                                                                                             | —                      | —                |              |
| 11 | **Double fabric max-packet size**     | Match the 1D fabric max packet size to reduce total packets sent.                                        | *Negligible*| TBD      |                                                                                             | —                      | —                |              |

Issue #24406: Expose Fabric Mux API via metal APIs
------------------------------------------------------------
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

Fabric mux functionality is needed for reduce scatter minimal.  We are using 2 workers per direction per link, and are using fabric mux to coordinate these 2 workers.

Currently the fabric mux functionality is only exposed locally within tt-metal, and we need it exposed to ttnn via the metal APIs so that we can create the fabric mux kernel and use it from a program factory.

### Describe the solution you'd like

When incorporating the example usage of fabric mux from https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/blob/main/tests/tt_metal/tt_fabric/fabric_data_movement/test_basic_fabric_mux.cpp into a standard program factory, fabric mux functionality such as tt::tt_fabric::FabricMuxConfig and tt::tt_fabric::FabricMuxChannelType are currently not accessible.  It would be good to expose these such that we can use/access them from within a program factory.

If it's helpful, you can see a desired usage in the branch jonathansu/ag-multiworker-per-linkdir, under ttnn/cpp/ttnn/operations/experimental/ccl/reduce_scatter_minimal_async/device/reduce_scatter_minimal_async_program.cpp.  On that branch I've modified CMakeLists.txt to pull in the necessary fabric mux functionality, but if those CMakeLists hacks are not included, you can see which references are missing.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24405: Create composite op to perform Sparse Matmul
------------------------------------------------------------
Mixture of Experts models may require matmuls where the sparsity of input may be around 3%. Pre and post processing the data should yield significant speedups.

A new matmul pybind endpoint should be created, sparse_matmul, and the functionality should be in ttnn/cpp/ttnn/operations/matmul/matmul.cpp (or other files in ttnn/cpp/ttnn/operations/matmul/).

Pseudo-code:
```
B = Batch Size
H = Hidden dimension size
S = Sequence length
K = Number of selected experts
D = Number of devices
E = Total number of experts

sparse_matmul:
Inputs:
RM token_buffer[B,S,1,H] - a token index is b (in range(B)), s (in range(S)) that specifies a single column (last dimension) in this tensor
RM metadata - bit mask (other options would be one hot encoding or list, would change approach and size, may be generated from other inputs like metadata tensor and mapping tensor):
[B,S,1,E/D] - 1 iff column in token_buffer at token index b (in range(B)), s (in range(S)) should be processed by expert e (in range(E/D))
TILE expert_weights [E/D,1,H,H]) - holds the expert weights
Output:
RM [E/D, B, S, H]
Algorithm:
for e in E/D
 for b in B, for s in S
  if (metadata[b,s,1,e] == 1):
   output[e, b, s, :] = matmul(token_buffers(b,s,1,:),expert_weights[e, 1, :, :])
```
If we assume S=1, B=8, and that big white rectangle is 3 entries, with 1 expert per device, 3 hidden dimensions, and all tokens and dimensions containing 1. Then this looks like:
```
token_buffer[8,1,1,3] = ttnn.ones([8,1,1,3])
metadata[8,1,1,1] = [[[[1]]],[[[0]]],[[[0]]],[[[0]]],[[[1]]],[[[1]]],[[[0]]],[[[1]]]]
expert_weights[1,1,3,3] = ttnn.ones([1,1,3,3])
for e in range(1):
 for b in range(8):
  for s in range(1):
   if metadata[b,s,1,e] == 1:
    output[e, b, s, :] = matmul(token_buffers(b,s,1,:),expert_weights[e, 1, :, :])
```
and ideally there would be the equivalent of two helper functions (possibly fused):
```
sparse_matmul_gen_input:
Inputs:
token_buffer [B,S,1,H], metadata [B,S,1,E/D], e (index of expert)
Output:
TILE packed_tokens[P,H] - packed_tokens[p,:] = token_buffer[b,s,1,:] where p is entries where metadata[b,s,1,e] == 1
sparse_matmul_gen_output:
Inputs:
RM metadata [B,S,1,E/D]
RM matmul_output [P,H]
e
Pre-allocated Output:
RM output [E/D, B, S, H] - output[e, b, s, :] = matmul_output[p,:] where p is entries where metadata[b,s,1,e] == 1
```

Then the algorithm would be:
```
preallocated_output = ttnn.tensor([E/D, B, S, H]
for e in E/D
 packed_tokens = sparse_matmul_gen_input(token_buffer, metadata, e)
 matmul_output = matmul(packed_tokens, expert_weights[e, 1, :, :])
 sparse_matmul_gen_output(, metadata, matmul_output, e, pre_allocted_output)
```

NOTE: to get good performance, it is critical that there are close to free views into the pre-allocated output and the expert weights. For the latter, that may require that to be an array/vector of tiled tensors. Good perf needs to be investigated. Other inputs may be acceptable, but warnings should be printed about potentially undesired low perf.


The following image shows the behaviour, and uses j instead of e:

<img width="1248" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/586e8eca-6d85-458e-acfd-64560c7ca5b5" />

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24399: Wrong output tensor shape from experimental.all_gather_async
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

The tensor ttnn.experimental.all_gather_async generates has wrong shape.

It looks like ttnn.experimental.all_gather_async doesn't do unpad at the end.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
import ttnn
import torch
def test_all_gather_async(shape, gather_dim):
    ttnn.set_fabric_config(ttnn.FabricConfig.FABRIC_1D)
    mesh_device = ttnn.open_mesh_device(ttnn.MeshShape(1, 8))
    try:
        input = torch.rand(shape, dtype=torch.float32)

        sharded_input = ttnn.from_torch(input, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT, device=mesh_device, mesh_mapper=ttnn.ShardTensorToMesh(mesh_device, dim=1))
        print("input shape : ", sharded_input.shape)

        compute_grid_size = mesh_device.compute_with_storage_grid_size()
        ccl_sub_device_crs = ttnn.CoreRangeSet(
            {ttnn.CoreRange(ttnn.CoreCoord(0, 0), ttnn.CoreCoord(compute_grid_size.x - 1, compute_grid_size.y - 1))}
        )
        semaphore = ttnn.create_global_semaphore(mesh_device, ccl_sub_device_crs, 0)

        sharded_output_async = ttnn.experimental.all_gather_async(sharded_input, dim=gather_dim, 
        cluster_axis=1, mesh_device=mesh_device, topology=ttnn.Topology.Linear, multi_device_global_semaphore = semaphore)
        print("output shape : ", sharded_output_async.shape)

        output = ttnn.to_torch(sharded_output_async, device=mesh_device, mesh_composer=ttnn.ConcatMeshToTensor(mesh_device, dim=1))

    except Exception as e:
        print(f"An error occurred: {e}")
    finally:
        ttnn.close_mesh_device(mesh_device)
        ttnn.set_fabric_config(ttnn.FabricConfig.DISABLED)

test_all_gather_async((1, 8, 64, 64), 0)
test_all_gather_async((1, 8, 64, 63), 0)
test_all_gather_async((1, 8, 63, 64), 0)
test_all_gather_async((1, 8, 63, 1), 0)
```

output:
```
input shape :  Shape([1, 1, 64, 64])
output shape :  Shape([8, 1, 64, 64])

input shape :  Shape([1, 1, 64, 63])
output shape :  Shape([8, 1, 64, 64])

input shape :  Shape([1, 1, 63, 64])
output shape :  Shape([8, 1, 64, 64])

input shape :  Shape([1, 1, 63, 1])
output shape :  Shape([8, 1, 64, 32])
```

### Expected behavior

```
input shape :  Shape([1, 1, 64, 64])
output shape :  Shape([8, 1, 64, 64])

input shape :  Shape([1, 1, 64, 63])
output shape :  Shape([8, 1, 64, 63])

input shape :  Shape([1, 1, 63, 64])
output shape :  Shape([8, 1, 63, 64])

input shape :  Shape([1, 1, 63, 1])
output shape :  Shape([8, 1, 63, 1])
```

Issue #24391: BroadcastHeightMultiCoreShardedOptimized doesn't work with 2D tensors and program cache
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

If I run eltwise operation (in my case it was add) which requires multicore height broadcast without program cache everything works normally, but if run a loop with program cache I get index out of bounds when trying to access H/W dim from input tensor.

Here is how we access input tensor a when program cache is disabled:
```
BinaryDeviceOperation::BroadcastHeightMultiCoreShardedOptimized::cached_program_t
BinaryDeviceOperation::BroadcastHeightMultiCoreShardedOptimized::create

    const auto& a = tensor_args.input_tensor_a;
    const auto& b = tensor_args.input_tensor_b;
    auto& output = tensor_return_value;
    auto bcast_math = binary_op_type_to_bcast_op_math(operation_attributes.binary_op_type);

    const auto ashape = a.padded_shape();
    const auto bshape = b->padded_shape();
    uint32_t N = ashape.rank() >= 4 ? ashape[-4] : 1;
    uint32_t C = ashape.rank() >= 3 ? ashape[-3] : 1;
    uint32_t H = ashape[-2];
    uint32_t W = ashape[-1];
```

and when program cache is enabled we do it without checking tensor dim:

```
void BinaryDeviceOperation ::BroadcastHeightMultiCoreShardedOptimized::override_runtime_arguments(
  const auto ashape = input_tensor_a.padded_shape();
  // indexing for H/W is invalid for 2D tensor
  uint32_t N = ashape[0], C = ashape[1], H = ashape[2], W = ashape[3];
```

When we run this program from cache we assume that we are running on 4d tensor, which my not be the case. In my case i was using 2d tensor.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

I didn't write repro intially since it takes some time (I hit this issue when running resnet through forge-fe) and I would have to translate part of resnet IR to ttnn to repro this. In order to avoid that I tried to root cause the issue.
If repro is necessary please ping me offline.

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Please complete the following environment information

Latest main

Issue #24387: Modify groupnorm unit tests to work on 8x8 grids on N300
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

This issue is to modify/fix these tests to run on N300 on the 8x8 grid.

Issue #24364: TTNN ToTensorSpec Operation
------------------------------------------------------------
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

TTNN ToLayout operation was initially created in mind of only allowing the change of tensor layout:
- TILE
- ROW_MAJOR

The current signature of the op in pure C++ is:
```c++
static Tensor invoke(
    const ttnn::Tensor& tensor_arg,
    ttnn::Layout layout,
    const std::optional<ttnn::DataType>& dtype = std::nullopt,
    const std::optional<ttnn::MemoryConfig>& memory_config = std::nullopt);
```


As you can see, there are additional optional arguments:
- dtype
- memory_config.

These arguments add to the operation semantics, mimicking a tensor spec change. However, the operation implementation only applies these changes if the tensor layout is also modified. In many cases, attempting to change dtype and memory_config together with the layout will fail, as a lot of combinations aren't supported by the op itself.

### Describe the solution you'd like

We strongly believe that functionality should be implemented where it naturally belongs. Since TTNN has the domain knowledge of which combinations of fundamental operations are valid for modifying tensor properties, we are proposing a new TTNN op `ttnn::to_tensor_spec` with the following set of optional arguments:
- optional tensor layout
- optional dtype
- optional memory_config

Additionally, to reduce user confusion and frustration, we should consider to_layout op only to change the tensor layout, without additional parameters like dtype and memory_config.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

In the tt-mlir compiler, we are modeling the to_layout op as a tensor spec op, but we are not mapping the compiler to_layout op to the ttnn to_layout op 1:1. To overcome failures with the to_layout op and its dtype and memory_config arguments, we are decomposing the compiler to_layout op into ttnn fundamental ops:
- to_device
- from_device
- to_layout
- typecast
- to_memory_config

The logic to handle that decomposition is very complex, and it comes from the ttnn op restrictions. For example, the tilization on the device only works if the data format is BF16 or F32. Another example is that the typecast operation only works when the tensor is on the device. There are many more examples, and all that complexity in the compiler is hidden in this ugly tt-mlir compiler pass:
https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-mlir/blob/main/lib/Dialect/TTNN/Transforms/TTNNDecomposeLayouts.cpp


### Additional context

_No response_

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24362: [Llama-3.1-8B][TG,DP=16] Model perf is below 2.5 t/s/u
------------------------------------------------------------
Llama-3.1-8B on TG, DP=16 has very low perf in Shield CI.
https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-shield/actions/runs/15920357153/job/44905747781#step:10:29


Issue #24360: Reduce redundant test cases in end-to-end model performance pipeline
------------------------------------------------------------
### Summary

There seems to be a lot of redundancy within the end-to-end model performance pipeline, and this combined with a shortage of machines for running end-to-end model performance means that the pipelines are long and have long queue times.

We should eliminate all redundancy within this pipeline, ideally only having a single test case for each model.

We should only need a single end-to-end performance test for most models. Ideally, we test the fastest configuration of the model (i.e. trace+2CQ) but not all possible configurations (i.e. trace and 2CQ and trace+2CQ). These other configurations can be tested in pipelines like "Frequent models & TTNN unit tests" because they have more resources.

### Models

I have collected the number of test cases for all models with more than a single test case. Here are all of them listed:

| Model | # test cases | fixed? |
|--------|--------| --- |
| models/demos/sentence_bert/tests | 2 | |
| models/demos/wormhole/resnet50/tests/test_perf_e2e_resnet50.py | 4 | ✅ (thanks @s-jovic) |
| models/demos/yolov4/tests/perf | 4 | ✅  (@esmalTT)  |
| models/experimental/yolov7/tests | 2 |  |
| models/demos/segformer/tests/perf | 2 |  |
| models/demos/whisper/tests | 2 |  |
| models/demos/metal_BERT_large_11/tests | 2 |  |
| models/demos/yolov9c/tests/perf | 2 |  |
| models/demos/yolov8s_world/tests | 3 | ✅  (@esmalTT)  |
| models/demos/mobilenetv2/tests | 3 |  |
| models/demos/yolov8x/tests | 2 |  |
| tests/ttnn/integration_tests/bert | 2 |  |
| models/demos/ttnn_falcon7b/tests | 5 | | 
| models/demos/vgg/tests | 2 | |

Issue #24358: MaxPool2d fatals in graph capture mode
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

When invoking MaxPool2d op through graph capture NO_DISPATCH mode (`query_op_constraints`) triggers following fatal error [code](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/blob/57fe73c861d9083b5abef3c029ada5af03d8d620/ttnn/cpp/ttnn/operations/pool/generic/device/pool_multi_core_program_factory.cpp#L639)

```
2025-06-30 11:07:50.008 | critical |          Always | Calculated CB size 14344 does not match with the actual CB size 64520   (assert.hpp:107)
```

Cause is most probably that code inspects state of the allocator which shouldn't be done in NO_DISPATCH mode. AFAIK in Conv2d op we worked around this problem. Look at the code snippet below
```
    // For now assume that if post_op_l1_allocation_size == 0 op is being run
    // in graph capture NO_DISPATCH mode.
    // ToDo: Device should offer an API to inform the op if it is running in NO_DISPATCH mode.
    bool is_graph_capture_no_dispathch_mode = post_op_l1_allocation_size == 0;
    TT_FATAL(
        post_op_l1_allocation_size == (this->pre_op_l1_allocation_size_bytes + l1_usage.tensor_allocation_size) ||
            is_graph_capture_no_dispathch_mode,
        "Mismatch!! L1 Allocation Pre Op =  {}, Post Op = {} Calculated Size = {}",
        this->pre_op_l1_allocation_size_bytes,
        post_op_l1_allocation_size,
        l1_usage.tensor_allocation_size);
```



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Unfortunately there is no MaxPool2d test in the `tests/ttnn/unit_tests/gtests/test_graph_query_op_constraints.cpp` but we should add one as soon as possible. It should be easy to copy/paste an existing test into a MaxPool2d test.

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24356: [BUG] Runtime exception when passing ttnn.CoreRange instance to ttnn.create_sharded_memory_config
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

Passing an object of type `ttnn.CoreRange` as the second argument in `create_sharded_memory_config` will always throw runtime error because the type `ttnn.CoreRange` isn't handled correctly.
signature:
```python
def create_sharded_memory_config(
    shape: Union[ttnn.Shape, Tuple[int, ...], List[int]],
    core_grid: Union[ttnn.CoreGrid, ttnn.CoreRange],  <--- this is where the problem is
    strategy: ShardStrategy,
    orientation: Optional[ShardOrientation] = None,
    use_height_and_width_as_shard_shape: bool = False,
) -> MemoryConfig:
```
The following set of statements is causing the runtime error:
```python
    if not isinstance(core_grid, (ttnn.CoreGrid, tuple, list, ttnn.CoreRangeSet)):
        raise RuntimeError("Invalid core_grid type")
```
at line no 134, 135 in ttnn/ttnn/core.py.

I got the runtime error while executing the following (written by me) code:
```python
#  Create sharded memory config
num_rows_per_shard = num_rows // num_cores
shard_shape = ttnn.Shape([num_rows_per_shard, num_columns])
core_range = ttnn.CoreRange(ttnn.CoreCoord(0, 0), ttnn.CoreCoord(num_rows, 0))
sharding_strategy = types.ShardStrategy.HEIGHT
sharded_mem_config = ttnn.create_sharded_memory_config(shard_shape, core_range, sharding_strategy, use_height_and_width_as_shard_shape = True)
```
If I change the `ttnn.CoreRange` in the Union to `ttnn.CoreRangeSet`, the execution proceeds further. Also, the `ttnn.CoreRangeSet` is handled inside the `create_sharded_memory_config`. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I got the runtime error while executing the following (written by me) code in ttnn:
```python
#  Create sharded memory config
num_rows_per_shard = num_rows // num_cores
shard_shape = ttnn.Shape([num_rows_per_shard, num_columns])
core_range = ttnn.CoreRange(ttnn.CoreCoord(0, 0), ttnn.CoreCoord(num_rows, 0))
sharding_strategy = types.ShardStrategy.HEIGHT
sharded_mem_config = ttnn.create_sharded_memory_config(shard_shape, core_range, sharding_strategy, use_height_and_width_as_shard_shape = True)
```

### Expected behavior

Since the `ttnn.CoreRange` is handled correctly for the core grid inside `ttnn.create_sharded_memory_config`, this function should accept `ttnn.CoreRange` instead of `ttnn.CoreRange` and there should not be any runtime errors doing so.

### Please complete the following environment information

- OS Version (Ubuntu 22.04 LTS)
- Python Version 3.10.12
- TT-METAL commit hash: 57fe73c861d9083b5abef3c029ada5af03d8d620

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24339: Migrate tt-train mesh mappers / composers to TTNN APIs
------------------------------------------------------------
APIs defined in this [file](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/blob/f5bf130165df4800788c6ecf0363260b3820c554/tt-train/sources/ttml/core/distributed_mapping.hpp) need to be migrated to the TTNN native solution. The replacement APIs are in [distributed_tensor.hpp](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/blob/f5bf130165df4800788c6ecf0363260b3820c554/ttnn/api/ttnn/distributed/distributed_tensor.hpp). (Note the composer interface is pending submission in https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/24317.)

There are several benefits: TTNN APIs support ND sharding, extend to multi-host, minimize the number of data copying, and rely on multi-threading to perform any necessary data conversion. And code deduplication; we would like to standardize the path to create tensors distributed across mesh.

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24338: Clashing dependency on timm library (tt-metal vs transformers)
------------------------------------------------------------
Huggingface added Gemma3n support ([blog](https://huggingface.co/blog/gemma3n), [v4.53.0](https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/releases/tag/v4.53.0)), and through it [timm](https://github.com/huggingface/pytorch-image-models) dependency, if the library is installed in the system: [gemma3n/configuration_gemma3n.py#L30](https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/a52478253bbe522a420e88ea3940d4d98a935300/src/transformers/models/gemma3n/configuration_gemma3n.py#L30)

In tt-metal we we are using [v0.6.13](https://github.com/huggingface/pytorch-image-models/releases/tag/v0.6.13), released on 2023-04-16 as a Python 3.11 hotfix from [v0.6.12](https://github.com/huggingface/pytorch-image-models/releases/tag/v0.6.12) from 2022-11-23. Here's the reference - ([requirements-dev.txt#L60](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/blob/main/tt_metal/python_env/requirements-dev.txt#L60))

Since in tt-metal enviroment the `timm` library is installed, the transformers library will try to import a class that exists only since [v0.8.10dev0](https://github.com/huggingface/pytorch-image-models/releases/tag/v0.8.10dev0) and this will crash. Just to mention, this version is from 2023-02-07, which is also quite old!

To keep up with the `transformers` library, we should update `timm` at least to [v0.8.10dev0](https://github.com/huggingface/pytorch-image-models/releases/tag/v0.8.10dev0). But since `timm` is still in active development, perhaps we could even update to latest? [v1.0.16](https://github.com/huggingface/pytorch-image-models/releases/tag/v1.0.16) got released last week!

The test that failed because of this is vLLM, as vLLM has a dependency on `transformers`, so it picked up the latest.
https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/actions/runs/15949479899

Thank you!

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24316: Eltwise (binary op) supports feature of automatic shard spec
------------------------------------------------------------
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

For some ops, there is feature to set  memory_config=ttnn.L1_WIDTH_SHARDED_MEMORY_CONFIG (or HEIGHT, BLOCK) without providing explicit shard spec, and expect op to generate automatic shard spec. Currently eltwise does not support it.

### Describe the solution you'd like

It may be nice to support it.

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

test case

def test_add_error(device):
    # Create input tensors with specified shapes
    input_shape = [1, 1, 1, 39576]
    bias_shape = [1, 39576]

    # Create random tensors
    torch_input = torch.randn(*input_shape, dtype=torch.bfloat16)
    torch_bias = torch.randn(*bias_shape, dtype=torch.bfloat16)

    # Convert to TTNN tensors with tile layout
    ttnn_input = ttnn.from_torch(torch_input, device=device, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT)
    ttnn_bias = ttnn.from_torch(torch_bias, device=device, layout=ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT)

    # Perform the add operation with the specified memory config
    ttnn_result = ttnn.add(
        ttnn_input,
        ttnn_bias,
        memory_config=ttnn.L1_WIDTH_SHARDED_MEMORY_CONFIG,
        use_legacy=False
    )

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24315: DRAM test sweeps hardcoded memory region
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

tests in `tests/tt_metal/tt_metal/data_movement/dram_unary/test_unary_dram.cpp` checks hardcoded memory region. Need to use HAL api to flexibly change the sweep region.
My previous PR which shrinks available memory failed. The test was adjusted by hardcoding, but need more flexible and portable method

https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/24013#issuecomment-3004833202

### Steps to reproduce the issue

_No response_

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Additional info

```shell
In this CI pipeline, there are 2 tests, test 1 - `test_llama_TG_perf_device` and test 2- `test_llama_TG_perf_device_non_overlapped_dispatch` ran back to back sequentially. There is a hang on running this in the order test 1 then test 2, however swapping the order (test 2 then test 1) removes the hang. 

You can see that both tests pass when the order is swapped in this CI run (only a measurement saving issue, irrelevant to hang) -> https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/actions/runs/15912162156/job/44882812330

Some additional findings tested locally:
- this hang was reproducible locally
- test 1 ran multiple times sequentially passes with no hangs
- running test 1 then tt-smi reset then test 2 runs with no hangs and passes
```

Issue #24309: The version of HF transformers that TT-Transformers depends on should track HF's releases
------------------------------------------------------------
Currently, the version of HF transformers dependency is fixed in `models/tt_transformers/requirements.txt`.

There is no question that we need this to support more and newer models. There are questions to answer about the implementation,  e.g.,:
- how often should the dependency be updated
- should we track the most recent release?
- what process do we follow when we have to use a pre-release HF transformers for a new model that we work on?

Main work being done in this issue: https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/issues/22607 (respective PR: https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/22608)

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24301: Query DST size from HAL
------------------------------------------------------------
There are plenty of examples of the following in the codebase
```C++
const uint32_t dst_size = fp32_dest_acc_en ? 4 : 8;
```

Can we get the HAL or device to expose the dest size, based on `fp32_dest_acc_en` and optionally `DstSyncMode`? 
Once this feature is in HAL, all ops that have the above pattern should be fixed.

Issue #24297: ttnn.prod lacks support for data types other than bfloat16
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

`ttnn.prod` fails to compute the full product reduction for data types other than `bfloat16`.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Script to reproduce the error

```
def test_reduce_prod_full():
  device_id = 0
  device = ttnn.open_device(device_id=device_id)
  ttnn.SetDefaultDevice(device)

  try:
      input = ttnn.full(shape = [1, 1, 2, 2], fill_value = 2.0, dtype=ttnn.float32, device=device)
      output = ttnn.prod(input)
      out = ttnn.to_torch(output)
      print("Input: ", input)
      print("Output: ", output)
      print("Torch.Output: ", out)
  finally:
      ttnn.close_device(device)
```

### Expected behavior

Compute the product reduction for entire tensor. 

### Please complete the following environment information

- OS: Ubuntu 22.04.5 LTS
- tt-metal commit: da0b85ea1b
- python version: 3.10.12

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24296: Optimize MobileNetV2 end-to-end performance
------------------------------------------------------------
### Summary

Since 86b608e303fb469b540baa52e444b8c5ae641127 the MobileNetV2 end-to-end performance is approximately 2590 fps but is 3000 fps on device.

Using the measured op-to-op latency, we can see the model is spending around 400 us on dispatch (this is a lot, this should also be addressed). If we add this to the measured device time, we get an expected end-to-end performance of 2665 fps. This is a gap of approximately 75 fps between the expected and measured end-to-end performance for this model. [Link to performance CSV](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1kjf1skRCAIJo8UOoNKL2bmkumo55EWU9g611xLUitRE/edit?usp=sharing).

The tracy profile shows that model runs are not perfectly overlap, likely due to the CQ configuration used. This is made worse because the output is tile format, so we have 22 rows of padding because the output tensor is `[10, 1000]`.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/05790b34-e7f7-4704-9be7-f1a253c89445)

The following things can be done to reclaim this 75 fps:
-  Switch to all input/output transfers on CQ 1 and model ops on CQ 0: doing this would probably get better overlap
- Remove padding from output tensor before sending to host
- Pad input tensor on device: we are currently sending 16 channels from host to device when we only need 3

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24295: Extend scatter write packet type to support up to 4 chunks (densely pack 4 bfp8 tiles)
------------------------------------------------------------
Some notes on implementation:
- The existing transmit/noc send code should be updated to use stateful APIs if possible. This should greatly reduce the CPU overheads of launching 4 back to back chunks.
  - Existing stateful options may not be sufficient but we should be able to cache fields that will be the same like cmd state.
- Additionally the primary use case for 4 chunk is that all chunks are the same size, so should, if possible simplify the implementation to support only 1 packet size, which will also have the benefit of making the noc payload size stateful.


Note: this feature is very beneficial for LLM prefill workloads (like llama 70b on galaxy prefill)

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24288: ShardedAccessor address calculation needs optimizations
------------------------------------------------------------
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

sharded_accessor.get_noc_addr(page_id) is very slow. As shown in [this table](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/10s6OmserfnaBcpp5erHliWZFbX4OXz6xwnqCK5j9cmc/edit?gid=0#gid=0) perf scales linearly with rank.  

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4b6dacdc-9976-497d-b0d1-922b88c652a2)

The example of reduce h written with nd shading  https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/23802 showed that almost half of time in reader kernel is spent in get_noc_addr(page_id): 
```
get_bank_and_offset: 	236 cycles
get_noc_addr: 		248 cycles
Inner loop scope: 	554 cycles
```

### Describe the solution you'd like

Ideas: 
- [x] Squeezing dimensions if possible
- [x] Add get_noc_addr taking coordinate as input
- [x] Simplify page_id->get_noc_addr calculation
- [ ] Tensor / Shard iterator
- [ ] Caching address
- [ ] Fast udiv (Granlund-Montgomery algorithm)

### Describe alternatives you've considered

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24287: ttnn.transpose doesn't work fully correctly for row-major tensors
------------------------------------------------------------
### Describe the bug

For tensors of rank higher than 3 and row-major, such as `[20, 10, 50, 10, 50, 10] ttnn.bfloat16` or.`[10, 50, 10, 50] ttnn.int32`, `ttnn.transpose(tensor, 0, -1)` doesn't work properly, causing a small percentage of data to be different from what it should be.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

### Example code:
```
import ttnn
import torch

dev = ttnn.open_device(device_id=0)

a = torch.randn([20,10,50,10,50,10], dtype=torch.bfloat16)
# or a = torch.randint(0,100,[10,50,10,50], dtype=torch.int32)
dim = 0
b = ttnn.from_torch(a, device=dev, dtype=ttnn.bfloat16, layout=ttnn.ROW_MAJOR_LAYOUT)
c = ttnn.transpose(ttnn.transpose(b, dim, -1), dim, -1)
torch.testing.assert_close(ttnn.to_torch(b), ttnn.to_torch(c))
```


### Possible result:
```
AssertionError: Tensor-likes are not close!

Mismatched elements: 67172 / 50000000 (0.1%)
Greatest absolute difference: 6.09375 at index (12, 6, 16, 6, 7, 6) (up to 1e-05 allowed)
Greatest relative difference: inf at index (0, 0, 11, 9, 4, 4) (up to 0.016 allowed)

# or

AssertionError: Tensor-likes are not equal!

Mismatched elements: 160 / 250000 (0.1%)
Greatest absolute difference: 1127989385 at index (1, 43, 4, 26)
Greatest relative difference: 1.0000001192092896 at index (0, 43, 4, 16)
```


### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Please complete the following environment information

_No response_

Issue #24251: New Fabric test infra features
------------------------------------------------------------
So far these are some nice-to-have features that can make the yaml more functional. Note some of them might already been supported and need further check. 
```
1. ftype is mcast, only specify core doesn’t do anything (has to also specify the address), we should be able to specify core only as well.
2. With multiple links, we can workaround by having multiple patterns per device, but how to select core by link is bit tricky. In exhaust mode, we need to fill one receiver core till max configs, but this will make the links using the same receiver core.
3. Ring support, currently missing, once we have that, we can start by specifying device by device the traffic pattern, later can solidify into half-ring, full-ring, and saturate-chip pattern (same as 1D test).
4. Solidify linear mcast (unidirectional) into a high level pattern
```

Issue #24239: Enhanced multi-device sharding metadata on Tensor
------------------------------------------------------------
TTNN allows partial tensor sharding over mesh; e.g. just 2x3 of a 2x4 T3K or by using 7 first row-major shards out of 8.

Metadata to track this information is currently limited to a list of coordinates:
```cpp
struct DeviceStorage {
    std::vector<distributed::MeshCoordinate> coords;
    std::shared_ptr<Buffer> buffer;
    std::shared_ptr<distributed::MeshBuffer> mesh_buffer;
```

Several related behaviors:
1. When allocating a tensor on mesh using `allocate_tensor_on_device` API, we populate all coordinates to be present. Technically, they store "garbage" data that was just allocated. When we write to this device tensor (`copy_host_to_device_tensor`), we replace these coordinates with what was actually written. 
3. When launching ops on multiple tensors (e.g. `ttnn::add(t1, t2)`), we validate and narrow down the set of devices to be launched on to the smallest set of overlapping coordinates that are present on all tensors. See code in `ttnn/api/ttnn/mesh_device_operation_utils.hpp`.
4. Similarly, the output of TTNN op will reflect tensor coordinates derived from the input tensors as per (2).

We need to extend this and make it easy to work with. In most cases, infra handles the complexity and does the right thing already, but CCLs need a way to query device "neighbors" in mesh, accounting for presence of the tensor data.

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================

Issue #24237: Add topology related queries in `MeshDeviceView`
------------------------------------------------------------
`MeshDeviceView` seems like a natural place to extend for getting information on the neighboring coordinates. Some of the methods need to be removed and replaced by ND equivalents:

```cpp
    // Throws if no device corresponds to `device_id`.
    [[nodiscard]] MeshCoordinate find_device(chip_id_t device_id) const;

    // Throws if the `coord` is out of bounds of this view.
    [[nodiscard]] chip_id_t find_device_id(const MeshCoordinate& coord) const;

    // TODO: #17477 - Remove the methods that assume 2D mesh.
    [[nodiscard]] bool is_mesh_2d() const;
    [[nodiscard]] size_t num_rows() const;
    [[nodiscard]] size_t num_cols() const;
    [[nodiscard]] DeviceView get_devices_on_row(size_t row) const;
    [[nodiscard]] DeviceView get_devices_on_column(size_t col) const;
    [[nodiscard]] DeviceViews get_row_views() const;
    [[nodiscard]] DeviceViews get_column_views() const;
```

Once we get a neighbor `MeshCoordinate`, we can translate it to `FabricNodeId`, and pass it down to kernels. 

One more nuance is that the `MeshCoordinate` might not physically be present on a Tensor (e.g. if it is sharded over a submesh or missing some shards). Need to take into account this when adopting the APIs in CCLs.

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24228: input_mask should be internally generated for ttnn.group_norm
------------------------------------------------------------
Currently for groupnorm (both the sharded and non sharded) there is an argument passed into the kernel called the input_mask.

<img width="1239" height="631" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f62e7ff1-6d4b-46dc-b548-5f98f27e5ccd" />

This is currently generated by ttnn.create_group_norm_input_mask. The code for this can be found at 

https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/blob/62ec08a9973f4eb1cbcdeadac7d917e0e756a615/ttnn/ttnn/operations/normalization.py#L207

The goal for a successful bounty completion would be to
1. translate the python code into C++ code inside of the groupnorm C++ code directory structure:
https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/blob/e35d4c1d128f39c8c010734f8fc9af01d708b3b3/ttnn/cpp/ttnn/operations/normalization/groupnorm/
2. remove the python code and replace it with a pybind pointing to the C++ code
3. call the new C++ code from within the op if the input mask is not provided 
4. add appropriate unit tests that call group_norm without the input mask (verifying that both the sharded and DRAM paths work, based on existing tests)

Issue #24217: from_torch / to_torch conversion should rely on device Ops as much as possible
------------------------------------------------------------
Currently, host is entirely responsible for converting logical data to physical data, which involves padding, dtype conversion, and tilization.

Instead, it will be much more performant to use on-device Ops for this, as much as possible:
* `ttnn::typecast`
* `ttnn::tilize_with_zero_padding`, `ttnn::tilize_with_val_padding`

The right thing should happen behind the scenes, without requiring any intimate knowledge from the user.

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24205: Consolidate Simulator and Silicon Flow for UMD Simulator
------------------------------------------------------------
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

Simulator flow currently assumes no ethernet cores exist, however control plane now probes for ethernet cores Cluster creation for both Simulator and Silicon flow.

* `get_active_ethernet_cores()` loops over the cluster descriptor and returns **invalid ETH IDs** if no ethernet cores exist.  
* Down-stream code (e.g., `is_intermesh_enabled`, probe logic in **DeviceFixture**) then references those IDs → crash/abort.  
* Failure surfaced in issue [#23832](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/issues/23832)


### Describe the solution you'd like

#### Short-term (guard-rail)  
PR [#24117](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/24117) – return an **empty vector** from `get_active_ethernet_cores()` when the SoC has zero ETH; mirror the same capability check in `is_intermesh_enabled()` and other call-sites.

#### Medium-term (unified flow)  
PR [#24007](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/24007) – run LLK tests on both silicon and simulator-SoC paths to ensure metal code works regardless of ETH presence. Follow-ups:  
1. Replace all hard ETH assumptions with capability checks.  
2. Keep simulator and silicon on a *single* code-path (no `if (isSimulator)` branches).

Example Locations of different code paths
- [Device Driver Creation](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/blob/main/tt_metal/llrt/tt_cluster.cpp#L329-L357)
- [UMD Chip instantiation does not enable ethernet cores](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-umd/blob/main/device/cluster.cpp#L159-L197)

#### Long-term  
Add regression matrices for future silicon without ETH (e.g., **P100**) *and* simulator with ETH.


### Describe alternatives you've considered

Currently we check for Ethernet core existence before calling functions related to getting ethernet cores. https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/pull/24117

 However, this solution is not sustainable since there are many locations where ethernet cores are assumed to exist.

### Additional context

_No response_

No linked pull requests found.

```
pytest  models/tt_transformers/demo/simple_text_demo.py
```
and I add a function in models/tt_transformers/demo/conftest.py to make default to start 'performance and batch-1', like this 
```
def pytest_collection_modifyitems(config, items):
    if not config.option.keyword:
        selected_items = []
        deselected_items = []
        for item in items:
            test_id = item.callspec.id.lower() if hasattr(item, "callspec") else ""
            if "performance" in test_id and "batch-1" in test_id:
                selected_items.append(item)
            else:
                deselected_items.append(item)

        items[:] = selected_items
        config.hook.pytest_deselected(items=deselected_items)
```

Issue #24184: ControlPlane renaming misleading intermesh APIs
------------------------------------------------------------
### Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe

**Issue**
We currently have the following APIs in `tt_metal/api/tt-metalium/control_plane.hpp`:
```
    // Check if ANY managed chip supports intermesh links
    bool system_has_intermesh_links() const;

    // Check if a specific chip has intermesh links configured
    bool has_intermesh_links(chip_id_t chip_id) const; 
...
    // Check if a specific ethernet core is an intermesh link
    bool is_intermesh_eth_link(chip_id_t chip_id, CoreCoord eth_core) const;

    // If the ethernet core is an intermesh link, probe to see if it is trained
    bool is_intermesh_eth_link_trained(chip_id_t chip_id, CoreCoord eth_core) const;

    // Query the local intermesh link table containing the local to remote link mapping
    const IntermeshLinkTable& get_local_intermesh_link_table() const;
```
This is misleading because these may not be intermesh (between meshes), but links to a remote host, since meshes can span across multiple hosts. 

**Proposal**
Rename these APIs (and some private functions and variables) for clarity:
```
    // Check if ANY managed chip supports interhost links
    bool system_has_remote_host_links() const;

    // Check if a specific chip has intermesh links configured
    bool has_remote_host_links(chip_id_t chip_id) const; 
...
    // Check if a specific ethernet core is an intermesh link
    bool is_remote_host_eth_link(chip_id_t chip_id, CoreCoord eth_core) const;

    // If the ethernet core is an intermesh link, probe to see if it is trained
    bool is_remote_host_eth_link_trained(chip_id_t chip_id, CoreCoord eth_core) const;

    // Query the local intermesh link table containing the local to remote link mapping
    const RemoteHostLinkTable& get_local_remote_host_link_table() const;
```

No linked pull requests found.

Issue #24177: Create public header for FabricNodeId
------------------------------------------------------------
**Issue**
The `FabricNodeId` class is currently `tt_metal/api/tt-metalium/routing_table_generator.hpp`

```
class FabricNodeId {
public:
    explicit FabricNodeId(MeshId mesh_id, std::uint32_t chip_id);
    MeshId mesh_id{0};
    std::uint32_t chip_id = 0;
};
```

As more and more APIs shift to use `FabricNodeId` instead of physical IDs (due to multi-host integration), as well as more usage of FabricNodeId, FabricNodeId should be in its own header public file.

**Proposal**

Move the declaration of `FabricNodeId` to its own header file:
`tt_metal/api/tt-metalium/fabric_node_id.hpp`
Move constructor and related functions (operator==) to its own cpp file:
`tt_metal/fabric/fabric_node_id.cpp`

Open to different names for the files.

fyi @omilyutin-tt @tt-asaigal

No linked pull requests found.
================================================================================
